description: Modest, sincere, learning-focused. Best for students with limited experience applying for entry-level roles.

examples.title: Humble Application for First Internship at Civil Service
examples.body:
14 March 2026

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to humbly apply for the internship position in the Human Resources Department at the Ministry of Health. I am a second-year student at the University of Zambia studying Public Administration, and I am eager to learn and contribute to your important work.

While I do not yet have professional experience, I have worked hard to build a strong academic foundation. I have completed coursework in organisational behaviour, public policy, and labour relations, achieving marks in the upper second-class range. I am also proficient in Microsoft Office and have basic knowledge of HR information systems.

What I bring is enthusiasm, a strong work ethic, and a genuine desire to learn from experienced professionals. During a group research project on public sector recruitment practices, I took responsibility for data collection and interview transcription for 15 civil servants. My lecturer praised my attention to detail and my ability to work collaboratively under deadlines.

I understand that an internship is an opportunity to grow, and I am fully committed to making the most of it. I am willing to take on any task assigned to me, from administrative support to research assistance, and I will approach every responsibility with dedication.

I would be deeply grateful for the opportunity to learn from your team and contribute, however modestly, to the Ministry's mission.

Yours faithfully,

Agnes Phiri

examples.title: Earnest Application for Library Science Attachment
examples.body:
2 June 2026

Dear Ms. Nkandu,

I am applying for the attachment position at the University of Zambia Library Services, and I want to share honestly why I am passionate about this opportunity.

I am currently in my third year of the Bachelor of Library and Information Science programme at UNZA. From my first day in this field, I have been fascinated by how information organisation and access can empower communities. I believe that libraries are not just repositories of books, they are gateways to knowledge and opportunity.

In my coursework, I have learned cataloguing standards, digital asset management, and user services. I have also volunteered at my college library during exam periods, helping fellow students locate resources and use the online databases. These experiences, though small, have confirmed my commitment to library science as a career.

I know that I still have much to learn, and that is exactly why I am seeking this attachment. I want to work alongside experienced librarians who can guide me, challenge me, and help me develop the practical skills that classroom learning alone cannot provide.

I am a quick learner, a careful worker, and I genuinely care about helping people find the information they need. I would be honoured to serve under your supervision.

Thank you for considering my application.

Yours sincerely,

Mumba Kaluba

examples.title: Humble Cover Letter for Teaching Practice
examples.body:
10 August 2026

Dear Head Teacher,

I am writing to request the opportunity to complete my teaching practice at your school. I am a final-year student at the University of Zambia, pursuing a Bachelor of Education in Mathematics, and I am eager to begin my journey as a professional educator under your guidance.

Throughout my studies, I have developed a solid understanding of pedagogical theory, curriculum design, and classroom management. My teaching method coursework has included lesson planning, assessment strategies, and differentiated instruction for diverse learners. I have also observed classes at two schools in Lusaka, which gave me valuable insight into the realities of teaching in Zambia.

I am aware that teaching is both a privilege and a responsibility. I do not take lightly the trust that parents place in educators to shape their children's futures. I am committed to creating a classroom environment where every student feels supported, challenged, and encouraged to reach their potential.

I am willing to teach any mathematics classes you assign, from Grade 8 to Grade 12, and I am prepared to contribute to extracurricular activities as well. I am particularly interested in helping students who struggle with mathematics, as I believe every child can succeed with the right support.

I would be deeply grateful for the opportunity to learn from your experienced staff and to begin contributing to your school community.

Yours faithfully,

Kennedy Mubanga
